NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 63007774)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT KAREN BANKHEAD'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS CORRECTLY CLAIM THAT THIS ACTION, AS ALLEGED (SEE PARAGRAPHS 7 AND 10), ARISES OUT OF ALLEGED "FALSEHOOD"/PERJURY. THIS ACTIVITY, IN THE ABSENCE OF FACTUAL DISPUTE, WOULD NOT HAVE BEEN COVERED BY ANTI-SLAPP. HOWEVER, IN THE ABSENCE OF A CONCESSION, AND BASED ON THE EVIDENCE BEFORE THE COURT, IT CANNOT BE DETERMINED AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT DEFENDANT COMMITTED PERJURY. FLATELY V. MAURO (2006) 39 CAL.4TH 299. THEREFORE, DEFENDANT SUCCESSFULLY SHIFTED ITS BURDEN UNDER THE FIRST PRONG BY SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITIES. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN UNDER THE SECOND PRONG. DEFENDANT'S ALLEGEDLY WRONGFUL ACTIVITIES ARE COVERED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE. HETERICH V. PELNER (2018) 20 CAL.APP.5TH 1132. CONTRARY PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT, EVIDENTIARY EXEMPTION IS NOT APPLICABLE UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THIS ACTION AS ALLEGED. AS STATED ABOVE, PLAINTIFFS CORRECTLY CLAIMS THAT THEIR CLAIMS ARISE OUT OF "FALSEHOOD"/PERJURY, I.E. THE CLAIMS "ARE PREDICATED ON A JUDICIAL STATEMENT OR PUBLICATION ITSELF" THEY ARE NOT MERELY USED FOR EVIDENTIARY PURPOSES TO SHOW INTENT. SEE OREN ROYAL OAKS VENTURE V. GREENBERG, BERNHARD, WEISS & KARMA, INC. (1986) 42 CAL.3D 1157. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; REPORTED: KIMBERLY JENNINGS, NO CSR#, 650.218.9855, KDJCSR@AOL.COM. (501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-15-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion Awarding Attorneys Fees To Defendant
Off Calendar Feb-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert Peterson, 415.586.7460 for Defendant/Moving party Karen Bankhead.
No other appearance.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT KAREN BANKHEAD MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion Awarding Attorneys Fees To Defendant is GRANTED in the amount of $10,156.25 (31.25 hours @ $325). Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert Peterson, III, 415.586.7460 for Defendant/Moving party Karen Bankhead.
No other appearances.
Reported: Kimberly Jennings, no CSR#, 650.218.9855, kdjcsr@aol.com.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT KAREN BANKHEAD'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Plaintiffs correctly claim that this action, as alleged (see paragraphs 7 and 10), arises out of alleged "falsehood"/perjury. This activity, in the absence of factual dispute, would not have been covered by Anti-SLAPP. However, in the absence of a concession, and based on the evidence before the Court, it cannot be determined as a matter of law that Defendant committed perjury. Flately v. Mauro (2006) 39 Cal.4th 299. Therefore, Defendant successfully shifted its burden under the first prong by showing that Plaintiffs' claims arise out of protected activities. Plaintiff failed to sustain its burden under the second prong. Defendant's allegedly wrongful activities are covered by the litigation privilege. Heterich v. Pelner (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 1132. Contrary Plaintiff's argument, evidentiary exemption is not applicable under the circumstances of this action as alleged. As stated above, Plaintiffs correctly claims that their claims arise out of "falsehood"/perjury, i.e. the claims "are predicated on a judicial statement or publication itself" they are not merely used for evidentiary purposes to show intent. See Oren Royal Oaks Venture v. Greenberg, Bernhard, Weiss & Karma, Inc. (1986) 42 Cal.3d 1157. Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Kimberly Jennings, no CSR#, 650.218.9855, kdjcsr@aol.com. (501)
